rulings.Case Title: JH CAPITAL PARTNERS II, LP ET AL VS. EILEEN GORDON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Respondents Eileen Gordon, Solo Io, Llc, And Gruppo Chiarello Incs Response To Petitioners Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Counterpetition To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday January 16, 2026, Line 1. Respondents Eileen Gordon, Solo IO, LLC and Gruppo Chiarello, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Seal Portions Of Respondents Eileen Gordon, Solo IO, LLC, And Gruppo Chiarello Inc.'s Response To Petitioners Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Counterpetition To Vacate Arbitration Award is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Respondents must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Ccp 664.6 Dismissal, And Enter Judgment Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday January 16, 2026, Line 10. Plaintiff Capital One, N.A.'s unopposed Motion to Set Aside CCP 664.6 Dismissal, and Enter Judgment Against Defendant is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as such: Plaintiff Capital One, N.A. shall recover shall recover $11,09844 ($11,632.44 less $534)14,149.09 in principal, and $597.00 in costs, for a total of $11,695.44. Prior to the time set for hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim. Prior to the time set for hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday January 16, 2026, Line 12. 2 - Andrew Chiu's Motion to Strike is DENIED. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
